title: Future Simple
definition: The Future Simple tense describes actions that will happen in the future — decisions made at the moment of speaking, predictions, and promises.

structure.positive: I will call you tomorrow. / She will help you.
structure.negative: I will not (won't) call you tomorrow.
structure.question: Will you call me tomorrow?
structure.whQuestion: When will you call me? / What will you do?

usage: Use Future Simple for spontaneous decisions (I will get the door), predictions (It will rain tomorrow), promises (I will always support you), and offers (I will help you with that).
examples: She will finish the project by Friday.
They will visit us next month.
I think he will win the match.

dailyConversation.speaker: B
dailyConversation.line: Yes, I will be free after 5 PM.

dailyConversation.speaker: A
dailyConversation.line: What will you do this weekend?

commonMistakes.why: After 'will', always use the base verb form, without 's'.
Don't add 'to' after 'will' in questions either.

quiz.question: Which signal word suggests Future Simple?

quiz.sentence: I will goes to the market.
quiz.correction: I will go to the market.
